title: philadelphus microphyllus var. pumilus
intro: Philadelphus microphyllus var. pumilus, also known as the dwarf mock orange, is a compact, shrub-like plant belonging to the family Hydrangeaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Philadelphus microphyllus var. pumilus include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy. Philadelphus microphyllus var. pumilus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
